ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees
Off Calendar Feb-28-2023 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees
Off Calendar Jan-17-2023 Continued To Feb-28-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom: Susanne Barbara Cohen, Esq. for David Levin (Petitioner). Sheila Robello, Esq. for Debra Dolch

Ms. Cohen stated that at the first hearing that the Court gave them more time to submit their fee request, that if they were paid by the agent that their fee request would go off calendar, and that Lise McCarthy is the agent and Franchesca Callejo is her counsel. The Court stated that nothing was submitted by October 19, 2022. Ms. Cohen stated that she emailed the probate examiner. The Court addressed Ms. Cohen stating that it had the probate examiner's notes and that it wanted to know if she was responding correctly to those notes. Ms. Cohen apologized if she did not respond correctly, stated that she would like to provide a status update noting that Mr. Shapiro had recently passed away, that they have been trying to figure out what to do, and that the fees were submitted to Lise McCarthy. The Court stated that there are threshold issues to be addressed. Ms. Cohen requested a short continuance to see if a probate petition would be filed, stated that they would file a creditor's claim in the probate, and noted that they had this unexpected development on December 28, 2022. The Court stated that the parties agreed to serve and that there wasn't a court order. Ms. Cohen provided a brief background stating that Mr. Levin was a neutral fiduciary that stepped in to serve, that he prepared the property for the sale, and that Mr. Levin was operating under a memorandum of understanding (MOU). Ms. Cohen noted that she is working with Ms. Callejo and Ms. Robello. Ms. Robello stated that there was a will lodged that addressed separate property, that a friend is named to serve re: Mr. Shapiro's estate, that Debra Dolch was not named, and that the friend/third party should be filing a petition for probate. Counsel confirmed that there wasn't a pour-over will, that there is roughly $200,000.00, and that the funds are mostly from retirement accounts. It was noted that Ms. Shapiro died last year. The Court stated that it understands that this is complicated. Ms. Cohen requested a six-week continuance, stated that the trust has the property, that her client served under the MOU, and that he is entitled to fees. The Court stated that the probate examiner's notes are fundamental, that the petition was going to be withdrawn, and that this can't continue. In addition, the Court stated that the only reason for this petition is the fees, that Ms. Dolch has already filed two accountings, that if there wasn't the remaining fee issue that it would take this matter off calendar, and that if the threshold issues are not dealt with appropriately that this petition will go off calendar.

The matter is continued to February 28, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. The Court stated that if this petition is going forward that the issues need to be cleared up. If the issues are not cleared up, the petition will come off calendar. The response to the examiner's notes is due by February 14, 2023.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Choi. Matter not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Susanne Cohen, via Zoom, for David Levin, former Trustee; Franchesca Callejo, via Zoom, for Lise McCarthy, Requestor.

The court and Ms. Cohen discuss the current issues with this matter. Ms. Cohen requests a continuance so that she can work on clearing the Examiner's notes. The court and Ms. Cohen discuss the pending accounting in this matter and the court states that it believes that Mr. Levin is not in the position to account. After additional discussion, Ms. Callejo advises that she is willing to discuss this matter with Ms. Cohen and that her client has power of attorney and may be able to pay related fees without court approval.

After additional discussion, the matter is continued to Oct-19-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cristobal. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Ststus On Filing Of Mr. Levin'S Accounting
Off Calendar May-02-2022 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Ststus On Filing Of Mr. Levin'S Accounting
Off Calendar Apr-11-2022 Continued To May-02-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Feb-17-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Vincent Yu appeared by zoom for Debra Dolch.

The court stated that it reviewed the petition and that is doesn't think it is going to be possible for it to take any action on it until it has an accounting from Mr. Levin. Mr. Yu stated that he reached out to Ms. Cohen (Mr. Levin's Counsel) to see if she would be available, but she said she wouldn't be able to be here. Mr. Yu also stated that in terms of their captain on the petition, his understanding is that it covers the period from when the accounts were transferred over to his client and that he doesn't believe there have been any objections to the substance of what they provided. The court stated that what Mr. Yu's client did seems pretty straight forward, that the position of the order wants it to acknowledge and approve the payments to Ms. McCarthy and that it is not sure it is something that would be appropriate. The court also stated that generally speaking it wants to do the accountings in phases, that the second accounting would be approved after the first accounting, noted that neither Mr. Levin nor his counsel are present and stated that it is not sure it is going to go along with it. Mr. Yu asked the court if it would approve his accounting if he struck the fees for them (Mr. Levin and Ms. Cohen) and let them deal with their fees. Mr. Yu also stated that he believes a declaration was filed regarding the $105,000.00 receipt, that the funds were not supposed to come out of that tax shelter vehicle, that it came out by mistake, that it was returned to a similar vehicle with a different institution, that they are expecting a response from the IRS and that it will be included in the subsequent accounting. The court informed Mr. Yu that is going to take out the part of the proposed order regarding Ms. McCarthy's payment. Status of Mr. Levin's accounting is set for April 11, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Yu is to inform Ms. Cohen of the status date. The Second Amended First Report and Account of Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: G. Bastain. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing The Bond
Off Calendar Jul-16-2019 Off Calendar

Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee, To Bring The Trust Under Continuing Court Supervision; Setting Trustee'S Bond And Authorizing The Trust
Off Calendar Jun-18-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sheila Robello for Debra Dolch (Proposed Successor Trustee).

The Court stated that there are a few issues with respect to whether notice should have been given to Mr. Der (Attorney for Trustor/Conservatee), whether it is necessary to have a separate declination from Mr. Goldman and the request for investment authority questioning why it should not be subject to the same rules and procedures as those governing conservatorships. Ms. Robello stated that invested authority is cited inside the trust itself. The Court stated that it has no problem with the existing investments, but if there are new investments, the rules would apply. Ms. Robello stated that she will withdraw the request and come in and request specific authority. Ms. Robello also stated that she reached out to all of the parties and there were no objections, that she did not have the opportunity to reach out to Mr. Der and have him waive notice, that because of the dispute between the parties she is asking that notice to Mr. Der be waived, and that the assets are the real property, and cash assets and investment assets other than IRA's. The Petition for Appointment of Successor Trustee, etc. is under submission. The Court will strike language from the proposed order with regard to invest authority. Bond is set at $771,000.00. Status on filing of the first account is set for July 28, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
